topic_title: EU AI Act — prohibited uses including social scoring and real-time biometric surveillance

required_sections.detail: Required sections 3 (Learning Objectives, ~80w), 4 (Introduction, ~150w), 5 (Core Concepts, ~900w across 5.1-5.6), 10 (Key Takeaways, ~130w) all present and non-trivial. Section 5 well above 100w; others above 50w. No placeholder strings in required sections.

citation_density.detail: Strict mode. Primary [1] (EC AI Act Service Desk Article-5 FAQ) cited repeatedly. Supporting [2] (artificialintelligenceact.eu Article 5) and [3] (EC Guidelines on prohibited practices / narrow biometric exceptions) both cited where used — [3] anchors the exceptions material in 5.4. Standalone factual assertions in sections 5/8/10 carry citation markers well above the 60% threshold. No fabricated markers: only [1][2][3] appear and all three resolve to resources.

forward_references.detail: All technical noun phrases resolve to 5.9 concepts_introduced (prohibited uses / Article 5, social scoring, real-time biometric surveillance, narrow biometric-ID exceptions, other Article 5 bans) or prior topics. Reuses 5.7 risk tiers, 5.4 four pillars, 5.1 failure motivation, and 5.8 high-risk obligations as contrast only. No reference to NIST RMF (5.10), White House EO (5.11), India MEITY (5.12), or framework-selection (5.13).

length_sanity.detail: ~1340 words, comfortably inside the standard profile range [1200,1600] from artifact-mix.json. Not >30% outside range.

topic_scope.detail: On-headline: unacceptable/prohibited tier, social scoring, real-time biometric surveillance, Article 5 bans, and the narrow biometric exceptions are exactly the topic title. High-risk obligations (5.8 — documentation, testing, human oversight) appear ONLY as a one-line contrast row in the 5.1 table and in 5.6, never taught at depth. That is allowed contrast use, not scope creep.

beginner_readability.detail: Matches artifact-mix audience baseline. Non-everyday terms defined plainly on first use (prohibited use, social scoring, biometric, real-time, Article 5). No paragraph stacks 3+ new terms (no jargon_dump). Short sentences, active voice, 'you' address; no hedging phrases (no academic_register). Zero distinct soft findings — no escalation.

verdict: PASS — Topic 5.9 corpus clears all six corpus-critic checks as a hard gate. Required sections present and substantive; web_search strict citation density satisfied with all 3 EU sources cited and no fabricated markers; no forward references to 5.10-5.13 (NIST/EO/MEITY/framework-selection); length ~1340w within standard range [1200,1600]; scope held to the prohibited tier + social scoring + real-time biometric surveillance + Article 5 bans + narrow exceptions, with 5.8 high-risk obligations used as contrast only; beginner-readable with terms defined on first use. No sections require revision.
